rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHANNA MARIE BAILEY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee Denise Rooke And Appoint A Neutral Court-Supervised Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/27/2026. Issue necessitating continuance: notice is insufficient. An amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53(a)). The proof of service attached to the notice of hearing filed April 2, 2026, shows that the petition was mailed on April 2. 30-day notice is required (Probate Code Section 17203). Please properly notice to amendment and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 05/13/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONARD RUSSELL MORECI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the "supplemental declaration" filed on 4/1/26 was not merely a supplement to the petition simply setting forth the legal description of the real property and disclosing that the administrator will be paying the attorney fees personally; it was the entire petition again. The bank account appears to be the decedent's still. Funds have apparently not been marshaled into an estate account. A supplement does not ordinarily require notice. [CRC 7.53(b).] A proposed order should not include orders that were not in the prayer.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AGONAFER SHIFERAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/26/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period. (LRSF 14.34.D). 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 05/12/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIVIAN L MACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/12/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A Court-appointed fiduciary, or a person seeking appointment as such, must supplement or amend his own pleading(s), not the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (2) Grandchild Tanika Mack was not given notice. No proof of service has been filed, showing that she received notice. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/28/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Issues should just be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not in response to issues posted in a tentative ruling or to "clear probate notes," which just highlights the fact that the petitioner and/or attorney failed to provide sufficient or correct information. Defective notice is cured by giving proper notice and filing proof of service of the proper notice; there is no need to narrate that it will be done.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA YING NORMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/2/26. (No earlier availability.) 1. A copy of Janet Yan Chiong's petition still has not been provided. 2. Notice was reportedly given to Jeffrey Robert Norman, who is deceased, in care of attorneys. The attorney represents the executor of Jeffrey Robert Norman's will, Dru Parker. Notice must be given directly to, or waived by, Dru Parker unless Dru Parker has directed in writing to send the notice to the attorney only. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b).] 3. The supplement AMENDED the petition caption. [See CRC 7.3 re definitions.] This is a petition for Letters of Administration, not Probate of a Will. (Missing information may simply be provided in a supplement; there is no need to recreate portions of the Judicial Council form.). 4. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. (Notice is not required for a supplement to a petition. [CRC 7.53(b).] However, notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is actually e-filed. A copy of the supplement was reportedly mailed on 3/12/26, but it was not e-filed until 3/17/26. Had notice of the supplement been required, notice would have been defective. If the "amending" language in the supplement is true, a proper amendment must be filed and noticed, including publication. [CRC 7.53(a).]) Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/22/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETER J. GABEL 2011 REVOCABLE TRUST DATED AUGUST 25, 2011
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets (Probate Code 850 A 3 B
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/9/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice mailed on 4/3/26 was not at least 30 days before this hearing. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Please file copies of the vesting deed and the bank statements immediately preceding the date of death to show how title is held. (Exhibits A and B were reversed... Probate Code Section 851 succeeded Heggstad.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/26/26. Notice with a copy of the supplement must be served. [CRC 7.53(b).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Only one set of courtesy copies is necessary.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ROOSEVELT JEFFRIES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARL LARRY SWANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing the final Inventory and Appraisal will be set for 9/22/26. Status hearing on filing either a petition for order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code 12200 et seq. will be set for 10/26/27. No appearance will be necessary if the required filings are made at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
